speaker: Jordan
text: Loyalty was killed by stagnant wages, mass layoffs, and the destruction of pensions, Taylor—not by Zoom calls. Treating the employer-employee relationship as a healthy, mutually beneficial transaction is actually a massive step forward. Employees *should* go where they are valued most. What you call 'shared purpose' was often just corporate gaslighting used to convince people to work 60-hour weeks for a company that would lay them off via an automated email. The new era of work is clear-eyed and honest. We trade our highly efficient, AI-augmented output for financial security, and we derive our 'shared purpose' and humanity from our families, our passions, and our local communities. Work is simply how we fund our lives; it is no longer the center of the human experience.
